From general knowledge: "Cage-free" and "free-range" are distinct labels with different requirements. Cage-free means birds are not confined in individual cages and have access to the interior of a building/barn, but **outdoor access is NOT required**. That distinction belongs to "free-range" or "pasture-raised" labels.

parsed.answer: No. Cage-free hens do not have guaranteed access to the outdoors. Cage-free means they are not in individual cages and have access to the interior of a barn or building, but outdoor access is not required. Free-range or pasture-raised labels have different (stricter) requirements for outdoor access.
